event: denied routinely irrigating or dilating neovagina
had penetrative sexual intercourse every couple of weeks with her husband
had not had intercourse in a few months
CT imaging of the abdomen demonstrated
extending from the pelvis to the lower abdomen
acutely worsened over the next 24 hours
presented to the local Emergency Department
increasing inflammatory process in the pelvis
transferred emergently to our children’s hospital
oxygen saturation of 95 % on room air
antimicrobials were empirically changed to IV ceftriaxone, IV vancomycin, and IV metronidazole
taken emergently to the operative room
diffuse intra-abdominal spillage of the mucus
about one liter of purulent fluid was drained
septic shock requiring three vasopressor agents
antimicrobials were transitioned to IV cefepime, IV vancomycin, and IV metronidazole
antimicrobials were changed to IV piperacillin-tazobactam
Infectious Diseases team was consulted for antimicrobial management
additional susceptibilities for the Bacteroides species were requested
experienced generalized malaise and diffuse abdominal pain
CT of the chest, abdomen and pelvis showed
bilateral pleural effusions with loculated left pleural effusion
multiple new abdominal abscesses, largest next to the liver (15.6 cm)
open anterior midline wound with wound vacuum
transferred to the intensive care unit (ICU)
underwent placement of a right perihepatic drain
drained 350 mL of pus from the right perinephric abscess
drained 90 mL of pus from her perisplenic abscess
Broad-spectrum PCR was sent on the drained fluid
antimicrobials were narrowed to IV ampicillin-sulbactam
discharge based on the susceptibilities from the original surgical cultures
Broad spectrum PCR from the fluid was positive for Gleimia europaea
(formerly Actinomyces europaeus), Alistipes onderdonkil, Varibaculum timonense, and Jonquetella anthropi
followed up at the adult infectious diseases clinic
with plans to reimage on day 60
repeat CT abdomen demonstrated decreased in the size of right and left sub-phrenic abscesses
transitioned from IV ampicillin-sulbactam to oral amoxicillin-clavulanate
for several weeks until complete resolution of the abscesses
